svg.title: 3D reconstruction from Hi-C as classical multidimensional scaling
svg.desc: A three-stage pipeline. Stage 1: a contact matrix C with values shown as a small heatmap. Stage 2: a distance matrix D obtained via d = C raised to a negative power, shown as the same matrix but inverted in tint. Stage 3: a polymer trajectory in 2D-projected 3D space, showing eight numbered bins as connected nodes with arc-like shape, A-compartment bins coloured cobalt and B-compartment bins coloured amber. Arrows between stages annotated with the MDS operations: contact-to-distance transform, double centring, then top-three eigenvectors. A side box lists the closed-form equation B equals minus half H D-squared H, X equals U-k Lambda-k to the one-half.

g.text: From a pairwise-similarity matrix to per-bin nuclear coordinates via standard MDS.
B = −½ · H · D² · H where H = I − (1/N) · 𝟏 𝟏ᵀ (double-centring)
Coordinates: stack the top-3 eigenvectors, scale by √(top-3 eigenvalues). The same routine recovers sensor positions from pairwise ranges and node embeddings from graph distances.
